```{julia}
  G = -(mu:*Z)'X
  Shat = ((y-mu):*Z)'((y-mu):*Z)*rows(X)/(rows(X)-cols(X))
  Vb = luinv(G'W*G)*G'W*Shat*W*G*luinv(G'W*G)
  *seb = (sqrt(diagonal(Vb)))'
  *b \ seb
  st_matrix("b",b)
  st_matrix("Vb",Vb) 
end
=#
#=
* Nonlinear IV estimator for Poisson: computation using optimize
generate cons = 1
local y docvis
local xlist private medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr cons
local zlist income ssiratio medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr cons
* Mata code to obtain GMM estimator for Poisson using command optimize
mata 
  void pgmm(todo, b, y, X, Z, Qb, g, H)
  {
    Xb = X*b'
    mu = exp(Xb)
    h = Z'(y-mu)
    W = cholinv(cross(Z,Z))
    Qb = h'W*h
    if (todo == 0) return
    G = -(mu:*Z)'X
    g = (G'W*h)'
    if (todo == 1) return
    H = G'W*G
    _makesymmetric(H)
   }
  st_view(y=., ., "`y'")  
  st_view(X=., ., tokens("`xlist'"))
  st_view(Z=., ., tokens("`zlist'"))
  S = optimize_init()
  optimize_init_which(S,"min")
  optimize_init_evaluator(S, &pgmm())
  optimize_init_evaluatortype(S, "d2")
  optimize_init_argument(S, 1, y)
  optimize_init_argument(S, 2, X)
  optimize_init_argument(S, 3, Z)
  optimize_init_params(S, J(1,cols(X),0))
  optimize_init_technique(S,"nr")
  b = optimize(S)  
  // Compute robust estimate of VCE and SEs
  Xb = X*b'
  mu = exp(Xb)
  h = Z'(y-mu)
  W = cholinv(cross(Z,Z))
  G = -(mu:*Z)'X
  Shat = ((y-mu):*Z)'((y-mu):*Z)*rows(X)/(rows(X)-cols(X))
  Vb = luinv(G'W*G)*G'W*Shat*W*G*luinv(G'W*G)
  *seb = (sqrt(diagonal(Vb)))'
  *b \ seb
  st_matrix("b",b)
  st_matrix("Vb",Vb)
end
=#

# `stata_poisson_gmm` (chunk 1) — the Julia counterpart of the Mata
# `pgmm` program. Nonlinear-IV (GMM) Poisson: moment conditions
# E[Z'(y − exp(Xβ))] = 0 with one-step weight W = (Z'Z)⁻¹, and the
# robust GMM-sandwich vcov V = (G'WG)⁻¹ G'WŜWG (G'WG)⁻¹ exactly as in
# the Mata code. `xlist` = private + the exogenous regressors; `zlist`
# = the excluded instruments (income, ssiratio) + the exogenous
# regressors. The endogenous `private` is instrumented; a significant
# difference from the plain-Poisson `private` coefficient signals
# endogeneity.
gmm17 = stata_poisson_gmm(df17, :docvis,
    [:private, :medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr],
    [:income, :ssiratio, :medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr])

# Bind the names the §17.7 two-step / overid chunks expect.
β_gmm17 = gmm17.β
X_g     = gmm17.X
Z_g     = gmm17.Z
y_g     = gmm17.y
xnms_g  = gmm17.coefnames
df17_g  = gmm17.df;
```

## 17.6 Stata resources

## 17.7 Exercises

```{julia}
#=
* Command gmm for one-step GMM (nonlinear IV) of overidentified Poisson model
gmm (docvis - exp({xb:private medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr}+{b0})), ///
 instruments(income ssiratio medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr) onestep nolog
=#
#=
* Command gmm for one-step GMM (nonlinear IV) of overidentified Poisson model
gmm (docvis - exp({xb:private medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr}+{b0})), ///
 instruments(income ssiratio medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr) onestep nolog
=#

# Stata's built-in `gmm` command, one-step, is the exact same
# estimator as the Mata `pgmm` program in §17.5.2 — moment condition
# E[Z'(docvis − exp(Xβ))] = 0 with the same X (private + exogenous +
# constant) and instruments Z (income, ssiratio + exogenous +
# constant), one-step weight W = (Z'Z)⁻¹. So `stata_poisson_gmm`
# (chunk 1) reproduces it identically (private ≈ 0.592, _cons ≈
# −11.86, robust GMM SEs).
stata_poisson_gmm(df17, :docvis,
    [:private, :medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr],
    [:income, :ssiratio, :medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr]);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Command gmm for two-step GMM (nonlinear IV) of overidentified Poisson model
gmm (docvis - exp({xb:private medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr}+{b0})), ///
 instruments(income ssiratio medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr) twostep nolog
=#
#=
* Command gmm for two-step GMM (nonlinear IV) of overidentified Poisson model
gmm (docvis - exp({xb:private medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr}+{b0})), ///
 instruments(income ssiratio medicaid age age2 educyr actlim totchr) twostep nolog
=#

# Two-step GMM — same moment conditions as the one-step fit above,
# but the second step re-weights by the optimal Ŝ⁻¹ estimated from
# the first-step residuals (Ŝ = (1/N) Σ (y−μ̂)²·z z'), which is
# efficient under heteroskedasticity. `stata_poisson_gmm` (chunk 1)
# with `twostep = true`. Estimates are close to the one-step fit; the
# SEs are the efficient-GMM ones. Bound as `gmm_ts17` for the
# `estat overid` chunk below.
gmm_ts17 = stata_poisson_gmm(df17, :docvis,
    [:private, :medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr],
    [:income, :ssiratio, :medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr];
    twostep = true);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Test of overidentifying restriction following two-step GMM
estat overid
=#
#=
* Test of overidentifying restriction following two-step GMM
estat overid
=#

# `estat overid` after two-step GMM — Hansen's J test (chunk-1
# `stata_estat_overid_gmm` reads it from `gmm_ts17`). With 9
# instruments and 8 parameters there is 1 overidentifying
# restriction, so J ~ χ²(1). A small J / large p means the extra
# instrument is consistent with the moment conditions (instruments
# valid); a large J would flag instrument invalidity or
# misspecification.
stata_estat_overid_gmm(gmm_ts17);
```
